# Retail Strategy Handbook

## Customer Churn
Customer churn measures the rate at which customers stop buying from a business. Retailers analyze churn by segment, lifetime value, service experience, and competitive alternatives. Retention efforts often focus on personalization, loyalty rewards, and superior after-sales service.

## Pricing Strategy
Pricing strategy defines how a retailer sets product prices to meet commercial goals. It may reflect cost structure, competitive positioning, customer value perception, and promotional objectives. Good pricing decisions improve margin while protecting demand.

## Omnichannel Loyalty
Omnichannel loyalty connects rewards, engagement, and experience across stores, mobile apps, and online channels. Customers expect consistent offers and service regardless of channel. A unified loyalty program can increase retention and improve customer lifetime value.
